module-5-customer-follow-up-capstone.label: Module 5 Customer Follow-Up Capstone
module-5-customer-follow-up-capstone.summary: Build a final customer follow-up report that reads like a polished handoff.

cp_3.title: Add the city and date focus

cp-1-start-follow-up-slice.title: Start the follow-up slice
cp-1-start-follow-up-slice.prompt: Begin the Module 5 Customer Follow Up Capstone. Return `full_name, city, status` from `customers` in that column order. Keep only rows where `status IN ('active', 'draft')`.
cp-1-start-follow-up-slice.hint: Use IN so the query keeps both statuses.

help.concept: Read the brief as a result contract: identify the source table or join path, the result columns, the row condition or grouping rule, and any required ordering.
help.hint_1: Build the result one clause at a time and run it after each meaningful change.
help.hint_2: Return `full_name, city, status` from `customers` in that column order. Keep only rows where `status IN ('active', 'draft')`.

cp-1-start-follow-up-slice.starterCode:
-- Returning full_name, city, and status from customers where status is either active or draft.
SELECT
  full_name,
  city,
  status
FROM customers
WHERE status = 'active';

cp-2-keep-real-contactable-customers.title: Keep real contactable customers
cp-2-keep-real-contactable-customers.prompt: Extend the Module 5 Customer Follow Up Capstone with the next approved requirement. Return `full_name, email, city, status` from `customers` in that column order. Keep only rows where `status IN ('active', 'draft') AND email IS NOT NULL AND is_test = 0`.
cp-2-keep-real-contactable-customers.hint: You need one extra result column and two extra filters.

help.concept: Read the brief as a result contract: identify the source table or join path, the result columns, the row condition or grouping rule, and any required ordering.
help.hint_1: Build the result one clause at a time and run it after each meaningful change.
help.hint_2: Return `full_name, email, city, status` from `customers` in that column order. Keep only rows where `status IN ('active', 'draft') AND email IS NOT NULL AND is_test = 0`.

cp-2-keep-real-contactable-customers.starterCode:
-- Keep the status filter above.
-- Add email to the result, keep only real rows, and require a real email.
SELECT
  full_name,
  city,
  status
FROM customers
WHERE status IN ('active', 'draft');

cp-3-add-city-and-date-focus.title: Add the city and date focus
cp-3-add-city-and-date-focus.prompt: Extend the Module 5 Customer Follow Up Capstone with the next approved requirement. Return `full_name, email, city, status, signup_date` from `customers` in that column order. Keep only rows where `status IN ('active', 'draft') AND email IS NOT NULL AND is_test = 0 AND city IN ('Chicago', 'Seattle') AND signup_date BETWEEN '2026-01-10' AND '2026-02-05'`.
cp-3-add-city-and-date-focus.hint: Use both IN and BETWEEN in the same query.

help.concept: Read the brief as a result contract: identify the source table or join path, the result columns, the row condition or grouping rule, and any required ordering.
help.hint_1: Build the result one clause at a time and run it after each meaningful change.
help.hint_2: Return `full_name, email, city, status, signup_date` from `customers` in that column order. Keep only rows where `status IN ('active', 'draft') AND email IS NOT NULL AND is_test = 0 AND city IN ('Chicago', 'Seattle') AND signup_date BETWEEN '2026-01-10' AND '2026-02-05'`.

cp-3-add-city-and-date-focus.starterCode:
-- Keep the follow-up slice above.
-- Add signup_date and focus on Chicago or Seattle customers in the January 10 to February 05 range.
SELECT
  full_name,
  email,
  city,
  status
FROM customers
WHERE status IN ('active', 'draft')
  AND email IS NOT NULL
  AND is_test = 0;

cp-4-finish-capstone-report.title: Finish the capstone report
cp-4-finish-capstone-report.prompt: Complete the Module 5 Customer Follow Up Capstone. Return `full_name, email, city, status, signup_date` from `customers` in that column order. Keep only rows where `status IN ('active', 'draft') AND email IS NOT NULL AND is_test = 0 AND city IN ('Chicago', 'Seattle') AND signup_date BETWEEN '2026-01-10' AND '2026-02-05' AND full_name LIKE '%a%'`. Sort the final result by `signup_date ASC`. Return only the first `5` rows.
cp-4-finish-capstone-report.hint: Use LIKE, ORDER BY, and LIMIT together.

help.concept: Read the brief as a result contract: identify the source table or join path, the result columns, the row condition or grouping rule, and any required ordering.
help.hint_1: Build the result one clause at a time and run it after each meaningful change.
help.hint_2: Sort the final result by `signup_date ASC`. Return only the first `5` rows.

cp-4-finish-capstone-report.starterCode:
-- Keep the follow-up slice above.
-- Keep only names that contain the letter a, then order by signup_date and limit to 5 rows.
SELECT
  full_name,
  email,
  city,
  status,
  signup_date
FROM customers
WHERE status IN ('active', 'draft')
  AND email IS NOT NULL
  AND is_test = 0
  AND city IN ('Chicago', 'Seattle')
  AND signup_date BETWEEN '2026-01-10' AND '2026-02-05';

try_module_5_customer_follow_up_capstone.title: Preview the Active Follow-Up List
try_module_5_customer_follow_up_capstone.prompt: Before starting the capstone, preview the customers who are ready for immediate follow-up. Return `full_name`, `city`, and `status` from the `customers` table, keeping only rows where `status` is `active`.

module-5-customer-follow-up-capstone.label: Module 5 Customer Follow-Up Capstone
module-5-customer-follow-up-capstone.summary: Build a final customer follow-up report that reads like a polished handoff.

cp_3.title: Add the city and date focus

cp-1-start-follow-up-slice.title: Start the follow-up slice
cp-1-start-follow-up-slice.prompt: Start the capstone report by returning `full_name`, `city`, and `status` from `customers` where `status` is either `active` or `draft`.
cp-1-start-follow-up-slice.hint: Use IN so the query keeps both statuses.

help.concept: The capstone starts with a broad but realistic customer slice you can refine step by step.
help.hint_1: Keep the selected columns the same.
help.hint_2: Use `status IN ('active', 'draft')`, then rerun the query.

cp-1-start-follow-up-slice.starterCode:
-- Returning full_name, city, and status from customers where status is either active or draft.
SELECT
  full_name,
  city,
  status
FROM customers
WHERE status = 'active';

cp-2-keep-real-contactable-customers.title: Keep real contactable customers
cp-2-keep-real-contactable-customers.prompt: Add `email` to the result, keep only customers whose `email` is not NULL, and exclude test rows.
cp-2-keep-real-contactable-customers.hint: You need one extra result column and two extra filters.

help.concept: A useful follow-up report keeps only records that are complete enough for real outreach.
help.hint_2: Keep the earlier status condition, then add `email IS NOT NULL` and `is_test = 0`.

cp-2-keep-real-contactable-customers.starterCode:
-- Keep the status filter above.
-- Add email to the result, keep only real rows, and require a real email.
SELECT
  full_name,
  city,
  status
FROM customers
WHERE status IN ('active', 'draft');

cp-3-add-city-and-date-focus.title: Add the city and date focus
cp-3-add-city-and-date-focus.prompt: Add `signup_date` to the result, and keep only Chicago or Seattle customers whose `signup_date` is between `2026-01-10` and `2026-02-05`.
cp-3-add-city-and-date-focus.hint: Use both IN and BETWEEN in the same query.

help.concept: This step narrows a broad follow-up list into a region-and-time-specific report.
help.hint_2: Use `city IN ('Chicago', 'Seattle')` and `signup_date BETWEEN '2026-01-10' AND '2026-02-05'` while keeping the earlier filters.

cp-3-add-city-and-date-focus.starterCode:
-- Keep the follow-up slice above.
-- Add signup_date and focus on Chicago or Seattle customers in the January 10 to February 05 range.
SELECT
  full_name,
  email,
  city,
  status
FROM customers
WHERE status IN ('active', 'draft')
  AND email IS NOT NULL
  AND is_test = 0;

cp-4-finish-capstone-report.title: Finish the capstone report
cp-4-finish-capstone-report.prompt: Keep only names that contain the letter `a`, order the results by `signup_date` ascending, and limit the final handoff to 5 rows.
cp-4-finish-capstone-report.hint: Use LIKE, ORDER BY, and LIMIT together.

help.concept: The final capstone step combines the core SQL foundations skills into one polished report.
help.hint_1: Keep all of the earlier filters and selected columns.
help.hint_2: Add `full_name LIKE '%a%'`, then `ORDER BY signup_date ASC` and `LIMIT 5;`.

cp-4-finish-capstone-report.starterCode:
-- Keep the follow-up slice above.
-- Keep only names that contain the letter a, then order by signup_date and limit to 5 rows.
SELECT
  full_name,
  email,
  city,
  status,
  signup_date
FROM customers
WHERE status IN ('active', 'draft')
  AND email IS NOT NULL
  AND is_test = 0
  AND city IN ('Chicago', 'Seattle')
  AND signup_date BETWEEN '2026-01-10' AND '2026-02-05';

try-module-5-customer-follow-up-capstone.title: Preview the Active Follow-Up List
try-module-5-customer-follow-up-capstone.prompt: Before starting the capstone, preview the customers who are ready for immediate follow-up. Return `full_name`, `city`, and `status` from the `customers` table, keeping only rows where `status` is `active`.
try-module-5-customer-follow-up-capstone.hint: Add a WHERE condition that keeps only `active` customers.

help.concept: This warm-up creates the active-customer query that becomes the starting point for the first capstone step.
help.hint_1: Keep `full_name`, `city`, and `status` in the SELECT list.

try-module-5-customer-follow-up-capstone.starterCode:
-- Preview the customers who are ready for immediate follow-up.
SELECT
  full_name,
  city,
  status
FROM customers;

try_module_5_customer_follow_up_capstone.title: Preview the Active Follow-Up List
try_module_5_customer_follow_up_capstone.prompt: Before starting the capstone, preview the customers who are ready for immediate follow-up. Return `full_name`, `city`, and `status` from the `customers` table, keeping only rows where `status` is `active`.

sketch_project_brief.title: Project brief
sketch_project_brief.bodyMarkdown:
A follow-up campaign needs a concise contact report built from the customer table.

Start with eligible statuses, then add contactability, location, date, name-pattern, ordering, and row-limit rules.

The finished query should be focused enough to use as a real outreach list.

sketch_project_brief.bodyMarkdown:
